title: Reducing Redundancy in Whole-Slide Image Patching for Scalable Indexing and Retrieval
abstract: arXiv:2606.26157v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: The rapid growth of digital pathology has created an urgent need for efficient indexing and retrieval of whole slide images (WSIs). This need is intensified by emerging generative AI workflows, particularly retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), which require dependable similarity search to support high-stakes clinical decision-making. Yet the substantial cost of high-performance storage limits the scalability and accessibility of WSI indexing for many healthcare institutions. Consequently, methods that can reduce storage demands while preserving retrieval accuracy have become a critical research priority. We propose ARReST (Antithetical Redundancy Reduction Strategy), a principled oppositional framework that leverages redundancy across dissimilar tissue classes to markedly decrease the number of patches that must be indexed from each WSI. Instead of eliminating only within-class duplicates, ARReST identifies antithetical patches-those

Title: Reducing Redundancy in Whole-Slide Image Patching for Scalable Indexing and Retrieval

This need is intensified by emerging generative AI workflows, particularly retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), which require dependable similarity search to support high-stakes clinical decision-making. translated_title: 面向可扩展索引与检索的全切片图像分块冗余削减方法
translated_abstract: 数字病理学的快速发展催生了对全切片图像（WSI）高效索引与检索的迫切需求。新兴的生成式AI工作流，尤其是检索增强生成（RAG），进一步放大了这一需求——它需要可靠的相似性搜索来支持高风险临床决策。然而，高性能存储的高昂成本限制了众多医疗机构对WSI索引的可扩展性与可及性。因此，在保持检索精度的同时降低存储需求的方法已成为关键研究重点。我们提出ARReST（对抗性冗余削减策略），这是一种原则性的对立框架，通过利用不同组织类别间的冗余性，显著减少每个WSI中必须索引的病理图像块数量。与仅消除同类重复块不同，ARReST识别出"对抗性图像块"——即那些在跨类别比较中具有高度冗余性的块，从而在保留判别性信息的同时实现存储压缩。
tldr: 提出ARReST框架，通过识别不同组织类别间的对抗性冗余块，在不损失检索精度的前提下大幅减少全切片图像索引所需的存储量。
en_tdlr: ARReST framework reduces storage for WSI indexing by identifying antithetical redundant patches across dissimilar tissue classes while preserving retrieval accuracy.
